routes.rope_note: 650ft alpine rock tower on generally excellent granite; single 60m rope for occasional belays/short raps off the tower. No confirmed fixed rap anchors — carry rap tat.
routes.corrections: No route-specific gear beta found online; based on SummitPost's description (5.8 rock/VI- alpine, 650ft granite tower, mostly excellent rock).

routes.rope_note: 4th-class arete/ridge scramble; a short rope may be carried for exposed sections, but many parties solo it unroped.
routes.corrections: Only a one-line SummitPost mention ('4th class') was found; no dedicated route description or trip reports located.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 walk-up/scramble via the south ridge; no rope typically carried. Occasional Class 4 terrain possible on the ridge's west side but avoidable.
routes.corrections: Matched to Mountaineers/Wenatchee Outdoors descriptions: Class 2 terrain, ice axe recommended for snow crossings; no technical rack needed.

routes.rope_note: 6-pitch, 500ft alpine rock route; single 60m rope. Upper section has newer bolted rap stations; original descent raps off a large chockstone.

sling_rack.cams: doubles 0.1-3in, triples in tips/fingers sizes if climbing near limit
sling_rack.nuts: full stopper set, used prolifically on crux pitches

routes.corrections: The matched route (Beckey-Beckstead 'The West Face', 5.11-, III, 500ft/6 pitches) is cataloged on North Early Winter Spire in search results, not explicitly tied to an area named 'North Peak' — flagging in case the area mapping differs. Grade (5.11-) is close to but not identical to the listed 5.11+.

routes.rope_note: ~5000ft alpine ridge traverse across North/Middle/Main peaks; single rope plus rap tat for multiple raps of varying anchor quality.

sling_rack.runners: handful to two of rappel tat/webbing

routes.corrections: Mountain Project route description explicitly calls for 'a single alpine rack and a handful or two of rappels worth of tat'; matches listed 5.7+ grade closely.

routes.rope_note: Single-pitch basalt route on the North Side of Pinto Rock; 60m rope sufficient to lower off.

sling_rack.cams: small rack supplementing bolts for knob placements

routes.corrections: No dedicated route page found; based on Pinto Rock's general area description and neighboring Sidewinder's confirmed single-pitch bolted format at the same North Side sub-area.

routes.rope_note: Single-pitch, slightly overhung sport route, bolts every ~4ft; lower from chains (anchor also used to toprope Top Gun).
routes.corrections: Mountain Project confirms grade (5.9) and 'sport bolted' single-pitch protection at Pinto Rock's North Side; matches listed grade exactly.

routes.rope_note: Single-pitch sport route toproped/lowered off the same chain anchor as Sidewinder, over a roof lip; bolt-protected.
routes.corrections: Confirmed via Pinto Rock area description that Top Gun is toproped off Sidewinder's anchor on the North Side sub-area. A separate, unrelated 3-pitch 'Top Gun' exists elsewhere in Washington — do not confuse.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 talus/scree scramble in the Glacier Peak Wilderness (Lyman Lake/Cloudy Pass area); no rope typically needed.
routes.corrections: No source specifically described an 'East Route'; other sources document only south-side and Cloudy Peak traverse approaches, both Class 2 with possible Class 3-4 on connecting ridges.

routes.rope_note: ~1500ft sustained Class 3-4 ridge scramble on dunite/olivine rock; rope sometimes carried for exposed sections, often climbed unroped in good conditions.

sling_rack.cams: a few cams/nuts for occasional protection
sling_rack.runners: webbing + rap rings for occasional rap

routes.corrections: Search results for 'North Twin, West Ridge, 4th class' almost exclusively describe North Twin Sister's West Ridge — likely the same real peak filed under a duplicate area slug ('wa_north_twin' vs 'wa_north_twin_sister'), matching the known WA duplicate-area bug. Gear mirrors wa_north_twin_sister_west_ridge; flagging for dedup review.

routes.rope_note: Class 3 scramble on grippy dunite/olivine rock, ~1500ft sustained; typically unroped. Ice axe useful on approach snowfields early/late season.
routes.corrections: Matches multiple sources describing North Twin Sister's olivine/dunite rock as grippy Class 3 terrain.

routes.rope_note: Class 3-4 ridge scramble, ~2000ft; trip reports show parties carrying a 70m half rope, harness, belay device, and prusik for harder sections/short raps, plus ice axe/crampons in early-season snow.

sling_rack.runners: webbing, quick-links/rap rings for rap stations

routes.corrections: Directly sourced from trip reports: 'ice axe, alpine harnesses, one 70m half rope, belay device, prusik, helmet, webbing, quick-links/rap rings, and a few cams/nuts.' Terrain can reach easy 5th class off-route, so many summer parties still go unroped in good conditions.

routes.rope_note: Presumed short trad route on Index Town Wall granite (Obelisk/Madsen's Ledge area); standard 60m rope to lower/rappel.

sling_rack.cams: standard Index trad rack up to #4 (Camalot-equivalent)

routes.corrections: Could not locate a Mountain Project or guidebook page for 'Lone Wolf' specifically. Area attribution to 'The Obelisk' at Index Town Wall is based on slug/proximity matching, not confirmed route-specific beta.

routes.rope_note: Class 2-3 non-technical scramble via PCT/south ridge in the Goat Rocks; well-graded trail to a short loose/exposed final scramble. No rope needed under normal summer conditions.
routes.corrections: Matches WTA/SummitPost/Oregon Hikers: 'nothing technical is required,' last ~100m is loose and slightly exposed but non-technical.
